posts.title: When the Daime came
posts.content:
In the Autumn of 2006, in our shared driveway one day, I was told by Sam, our neighbor of six years, that there was to be a ceremony there that coming weekend.  He wanted to be sure  we wouldn’t be trapped behind all the cars that would be there on Saturday.  I thought not much of it, though was curious. I thought maybe he and his girlfriend Tara were going to be wed… Good news!

He then told me that it may be something that interests me. “Have you heard of Ayahuasca?” he asked. “Yes!” I replied with eager interest. “Well, it’s a ceremony that is Christian but that has as its sacrament, Ayahuasca.” I wished right then that we didn’t have plans for the weekend. “I would Love to participate,” I expressed, extremely curious about how Christianity and tribal ritual would get along. Also really interested and excited that the opportunity to commune with Ayahuasca had just presented itself to me. “I’ll let you know when it happens again then.”

It had been a time of mending at home. Laura and I had been through some intense tumult over the last year and were seeing our lives stabilize a little bit, though still involved in big work being together. The house we lived in was no longer an adequate abode for our growing family and the thought of another winter in it was chilling. We have been loosely formulating a life plan for ourselfs since we’ve met and it seemed at that point that we were still far from anything concrete other than ‘going with the flow’. We couldn’t have suspected then, where that flow was to take us in the coming months.

I eagerly told Laura about what had happened and she expressed cautious curiosity. About a month later, timing worked out that we had a weekend free in sync. with a ceremony work. I was finally going to get to commune with Ayahuasca, The Sacred Potion of the Amazon.

posts.title: A Little Bit of His Story
posts.content:
Like a significant number of my contemporaries, I was born on drugs. I don’t know what drug they gave my mom when I was coming out breached but it certainly stoned her. She was also on nicotine and I believe caffeine too. Perhaps it may not be that important, but then again, it may be crucial information as I further learn to understand what goes on inside of me…

I’ve been a deeply spiritual being for as long as I can remember. Growing up, I was fortunate to have had abundant natural environments around me and have cultivated an understanding of them that goes beyond deep appreciation and reverence. It’s a connection that I’m unable to describe as anything but spiritual. Plants speak volumes to me all the time, and the place I feel most at home is among the plants and other energies that exist in a natural ecosystem.

Officially, I was raised Christian. Roman Catholic, to be specific. We weren’t an especially devout family but attended church most Sundays. My mom Loved to sing and enjoyed the ritual of the mass. I was an altar boy too.

One day at mass, I must have been twelve or thirteen. I clearly remember looking up at my mother during one of the prayers (we went to church in French, not my mother tongue but a language with which I am fluent) and said to her “Mommy this is brainwashing,” to which she replied “No it isn’t honey”. That wasn’t a good enough answer for a curious pre-teen intellectual. My disconnection with the Catholic Church began right about then.

Things changed drastically for me after my parents separated when I was thirteen. My relationship with the stable social structures around me began its deterioration then. If I can draw a positive out of a traumatic situation it’s that it gave me a less firm attachment to things taken for granted; a tool that has helped me tremendously on my spiritual journey.

I first communed with cannabis in my late teens. I smoked hash from a pin with my step-brother to negligeable results. Then I ate some hash and threw up after becoming INCREDIBLY stoned. This was a pretty big AHA! moment for me. That such a small piece of something could have made me feel such incredible sensations transformed my understanding of the plant world. The fact that this connection with bliss was illegal transformed my relationship with our society in general.

I spent my late teens and into my twenties learning to find forgiveness and compassion for my attempted indoctrination into the Catholic Church. Still longer to find the same in relation to my indoctrination and inheritance of such a messed up modern world (I’m still working on it). It took a lot of effort to realize that folks use their religions for a lot of the same reasons that i used cannabis and other allies: namely LSD and psilocybin mushrooms. That we are all seeking in some way or another and we all have our own unique toolkit.

When I was twenty three, I was introduced to the work of Terence Mckenna. His fantastical ideas about humanity and its relationship with plants astounded me. His theories about the nature of time and existence, I’m still trying to encompass and integrate. It was here that I learned of the concept of ritualistic use of entheogens. It was here where I first read about Ayahuasca.

From then ‘trips’ with mushrooms or LSD took on a completely different meaning for me. I began to notice that recreational use of these substances, while amusing and often enlightening in ways, was only scratching the surface of what was really available to me through careful study and conscious communion.

I have come to realize that this study is a project that is going to take a lifetime, as all true spiritual journeys do.  And that it is THE project for this lifetime.

Recently this trip has brought me to begin some sort of chronicle, because I’ve found it to be getting really interesting all of a sudden and I feel that if the whole world could get it like I’ve been getting it as of late, then we’ll be a much kinder, gentler humanity…

posts.title: Drips Subtleties
posts.content:
This nightscape
It
Drips
Subtle
Phenomena
From its inky star-speckled depths:

Insight and
Resonance
And Light and

I’m just sitting here,
Skipping stones of unlimited wonder
Across a vast, everwaving wave of ‘what if?’
Into…

…

…

this

Nightscape
Drips subtleties
Into the quiet space of mind
Onto its reflection for a
Fast refraction back
To understand it
In a way that
Dayscape
Seems to
Lack.

This

Tool of
Understanding
I wield from space of mind
Of openness and viscous
Clarity, as I can
Be it, with all
That we’ve
Created to
Distract
From…

…

…

this.

posts.title: First Daime
posts.content:
There were about 25 of us there that first time. A Daime ceremony is referred to as a ‘Work’ by its participants and the term is well suited for what goes on. We congregated around eleven in the morning with people arriving over about an hour. It was a really celebratory and excited environment as folks scurried around the house connecting and getting ready for the work.

The ceremony is quite something, really. Congregations range in size from several hundred people to less than ten. Its around six hours long and it’s mostly filled with song. There are a couple of ‘concentrations’~quiet meditation, and a couple of breaks, but other than that it’s all singing… in Portuguese.

The hymns are really beautiful songs. There are hundreds of them praising things as varied as hummingbirds, the spirits of the forest, Daime (the ayahuasca brew), our humanity and personal process, Jesus, Mary and Joseph, St. John the baptist, and others. This time, there was a guitar and clarinet to accompany as well as several folks rattling shakers.

The congregation is segregated: the women and men on separate sides of the room. Twelve Fardado (initiated members of the Church) sit at a central table adorned with candles, fresh flowers, crystals, photographs and other personal items in a communal altar. The rest of us are sitting around them. In every corner of the room is a mattress and cozy place for those who need to lay down for some really deep work.

We are all welcomed to the work, especially the new folk(there are four of us, I Think). And we begin: After opening prayers of ‘The Lord’s Prayer’ and ‘Hail Mary’ and others, we drink the sacrament. It was poured By Horatio, the Padrinho, into little plastic cups from a lovely blue vase. The drink is an infusion made from a vine: Banisteriopsis Caapi (B. Caapi), and a shrub called Chakruna (psychotria virdis) from Brazil. In Tribal Brazil they call this brew Ayahuasca and Yage. In Santo Daime they call it Daime.

This is what happened:

After the first drink…about a quarter of a small plastic cup…. maybe an eighth of a cup in volume.

Remarkable flavor. Almost like a gum that I’ve had before. Goes in really acrid but then it’s a smorgasborg of flavor: The murky light brown thin liquid was very bitter and sour then sweet and bitter and….. very complexe… It sticks in the mouth too.

Then the singing began. Sometimes we stood but mostly we sat. After about a half hour, I began feeling the physical sensation of tryptamine ‘intoxication’. For those familiar with psilocybin mushrooms, it feels something like that: slight belly rumbles, general ease of being, relaxed muscles and happy… I feel the unmistakable feeling the of enhanced perception kick in.

After singing for about an hour, and another half-hour of silent ‘concentration’, we drink again.

The second drink. I asked Horatio for a good dose. It was maybe double the first amount… or not quite.

Acceleration of the physical ‘buzz’. Muscles are kinda jelly-feeling. A couple of really satiating yawns. Very heightened sense perception. What I’ve dubbed as ‘The Oneness Sensation’ swooshes over me, repeatedly. It’s a feeling of connectedness with the universe. Like actually understanding this completely with the body in those moments. It’s unlike any sensation I’ve felt, and I’ve felt it only through communion with tryptamines. Quite a satisfying feeling, and if that’s all that tryptamines were good for, it would still be enough.

The ‘trip’ so far though, is not much different than the psilocybin buzz. The visuals are a little bit different in their manifestation; they seem not as geometrical and not as much in motion; more like working the ‘primal matter’ into scenes. I’m feeling a little disappointed at this point: having been communing for about three hours and not feeling anything much more than a mushroom buzz. I commented to a fellow outside on a break that it felt that an appropriate thing to be doing would be hunting . I felt like I wanted to track some prey.

After a third drink, about as much as the second–maybe a little less–I was starting to feel an acceleration in what was going on in me. The oneness sensation is almost overwhelmingly really there, pulsing rapidly and I’m feeling like I’d really like to be alone; to sit and listen to what this stuff has to say to me rather than what the congregation has to say. It feels noisy and busy and very swooshyswirly.

The Remains of the pitcher are offered and I’m able to have another sip. About half as much as the first time.

There’s one more set of 12 hymns to be sung and then closing hymns: three songs. I’ve been spending most of the ceremony sitting in a chair, leaning on my knees which I’ve propped up. I’ve been looking at candles on the table and looking outside and closing my eyes; observing the waves of energy in the room and watching people’s connection with it. I’m now sitting and feeling pretty intense waves of energy flowing through me. It’s becoming too intense though and I’m starting to feel like I have to vomit, (it’s not abnormal to vomit at these things… but I don’t want to. “I’ve made it this far…”) I resist as far as I can, and while this is going on, I’m losing track of the physical world around me. It kind of feels like a CD skipping. The room is skipping in and out of now. I’m feeling a slight panic so I hurriedly decide to get up and go to the washroom.

The next thing, one of the other men is looking at me really intensely, RIGHT in my face saying “Do you want to lay down? Do you want to lay down?” The second time it was real and his eyes were so rapid (felt almost like a synapse, a snapshot) it seemed like a good idea to lay down so I shouted “YES!” to him. Then the world left again and I’m not been able to fully make understood what happened next.

I’ll try.

It’s like I stepped onto some sort of conveyor belt that existed out of time. The world around me was very graphic, not of this world and moving by my consciousness so fast that I don’t retain a lot of specific images. (though I can still see them all now in my minds eye, I couldn’t describe them if I tried!). It was quiet in this place, with perhaps a trace of white noise. I was in it as myself being in it and feeling a sense of awe and pleasant confusion. It felt like forever in awe between agreeing to lay down and feeling my face in the pillow, though it couldn’t have taken more than a few seconds. Then as i was being told that it was a good idea to lay on my back instead of front, and I turn over, there’s an

orb

A giant and majestic orb made of soft and bright white light taking up most of my field of vision. Around the circumference of this orb, there are bolts of lightning shooting out from the top and down, both clock and counter-clock wise. The bolts are symmetrical and rhythmic, mostly in pairs and flashing a purple and green. The scene before me was very fast, like instant, yet went on for what seemed to be several minutes.

Then it was over. This reality skipped back in and I was laying in my body again and the ceremony was going on. I’m feeling in such a peaceful state. Stunned, but at ease and so pleased. The guy comes and checks on me, asks if I’m o.k. and I ask where my glasses are. They’re on the bookcase beside me and “yes I’m o.k.”. I’m a little bit confused but I’m very very blissfully o.k…

I stood up with everyone else for the closing hymns and prayers. As it was ending, my senses were returning to ‘normal’: still trippy but ‘in control’. Then the ceremony was over in a moment of jubilation. Hugs abound the room and congratulations. Then we drifted out of the room and feasted together.

I asked the guy right after the ceremony, because I had to know what the situation was that caused him to be suddenly right in my face asking me if I wanted to lay down; I had simply decided to go to the washroom. What happened was this: Over the course of several minutes, I slumped forward in my chair and then fell forward onto the floor, forehead first. Unconscious. I was on the ground for no more than five minutes when he was in my face. My eyes were open the whole time.

Where I went to is such a curious and compelling thing to me… and perhaps, the answer to the question of “Life, the Universe and Everything”. The sensation of time, or timelessness (or…) was something I’m unable to completely describe. The visual scape that presented itself around me was astonishing and full of information, motion, and a reality that was very removed from this place.

That night, I went home to my family and found myself completely transformed, having just had a profoundly religious experience. One that has yet to end.

posts.title: Impressions
posts.content:
HOLY SHIT!

I’ve found

IT!

No, really though.

For at least a week after that first ceremony I found that I existed in a place that was out of this world. Otherworldly is a term I’ve used to describe it. It’s a very serene and meditative place; a really sacred space. It felt like I was vibrating at a very different frequency than before I went in. Sensations, both physical and emotional were more concretely felt. Not only that, I understood motivational forces more clearly, in myself as well as in others. The telepathic relationship that I share with my son was more glaringly obvious and I could now more clearly see it as it was going on; on some indescribable level, I could understand how it was going on.

Gradually this feeling waned back into this regular old filter of this reality as a paradigm but the memory and mere knowledge of it existing has been enlightening and continues still to this day.

As far as my more ‘scientific’ exploration of the experience, the IT that I found is the state of being that exists outside of normal perceptions~~outside of the body it seems. I’ve read about it for years and attempted to access it with heroic doses of psilocybin mushrooms (10+grams for a trip) but had yet to discover it before this. I now know what all the psychonauts have been talking about.

Psychologically, I have felt the sensation of ego loss. The intensity of the experience came from me not knowing how to let go of the ego and because I didn’t know how to get there, the Daime had to knock me out to get me there, saying: “Oh jeez, MyKL! if you don’t let go, we can’t do this… HERE!” I am convinced that this as a regular practice will teach me how i can do that more easily. With or without the Daime.

As far as plant allies go, I feel quite impressed with ayahuasca. By far the most immediately transformative experience of my life, the ripples of which I’m feeling still in my daily life. I feel more safe in the world after having seen that place, yet the world seems to have noticeably quickened around me. ‘Coincidental’ connections are showing themselves to me more readily while other important life connections are becoming more clear all the time. I have lost any fear or concern around the idea of dying. Death seems less a mystery and more of an adventure… we’ll see then won’t we. ;o)

My first journey with Daime has excited me in ways that i haven’t felt before. It’s akin to the potency of falling in Love. Yes it’s

THAT BIG.

As for the ceremony itself: it’s gruelling. A very challenging and difficult thing to do to one’s self. Definitely not for the faint of heart. It is in this sense that I immediately understand what they mean when they call it ‘work’ oof!

I think about ‘spirituality’ and ‘religion’ as two distinctly different things. As Neale Donald Walsh put it nicely: “There are a million paths to God (spirituality) and religion is only one of them.” It seems, through all the learning I’ve done(which isn’t vast but is significant), that a big part of what we’re doing in this is to learn how to die. The ideas of ‘heaven’ and ‘hell’, incarnations, transcendence and enlightenment, and the processes of achievement of these appear to be connected with the ‘afterlife’ state.

It seems also unlikely that there is nothing after the end of this physical body. We’ve been talking about it since before we could write and still it remains a mystery for those of us still embodied. I believe that the Daime has offered me an important clue to all of it. A most profound AHA! moment.

posts.title: Who Have We Been Trying to Fool?
posts.content:
Who have we been trying to fool?

I’d been feeling concerned that I might not ever see
The Truth that’s hiding in the trees
Seas, herbs and planet divine.
With our dominator culture it’s so hard to find

That vibe, so clear, unmaligned by technology
And by notions of modernism, the perceived end seems silly.
It speaks with bizarre language, vague to sensibilities,
Touting the now present paradigm of disconnection for my society.

And so I’ve found a connection with infusions and ferments
And through compounds found in sacred smoke and fungus flesh
To help learn what I’d been missing, what I hadn’t yet been taught
By the agents that seek to eliminate the Truth our world wrought.

posts.title: Second Daime
posts.content:
Dear Horatio,

I went into it the second time naively thinking that what happened to me the first time was going to happen every time. I suppose that if I give this enough work, it may happen again. The journey on the way to getting there is certainly worth it for all of the other places that I go with this. The Daime experience is a marvelous and magical tool.

The deepest thing for me about the event this Saturday was the experience after the ceremony proper. The ceremony had its own wonderful depth that I’m still exploring and working with, but after, while sitting with the others I was enchanted by the descriptions you were sharing from your experiences of letting go of the body.

That was profound for me to take in, and I left my body shortly after you spoke of it. I entered a trance, staring ahead of me at the floor in front of where Arden sat. A serene peaceful place, nothing really happening, somewhat of a void; the peaceful void. The same sensation of instantaneous eternity was there as what happened with my first Daime. When I snapped back to this world Arden was gone. It was very bizarre and it left me wondering how long I had spent away.

Having this happen has awakened, upon retrospection today, some memory of the experience my first time: in that, the instant between deciding to go to the bathroom and having Hans so gloriously in my face was also an eternity, vast like the one on the way to laying down… part of the same one, I’m guessing. I can relate to the feeling of watching your body fall after touching the tree with your forehead at the Sun Dance(or did you really run into it full-on like the rest of them-only all you saw was the kiss…?…). I didn’t see or feel my body fall to the ground but what I thought I did and what my body did were two different things.

This time at the ceremony it was almost like all the things that came to me were messages created in/by the memory of that space. Very potent and fulfilling. Sometimes it was visual scenes painted on my mind’s eye, other times it was feelings, touching on grand emotional states but still well connected to reality to not let go completely.

The most profound moment of the ceremony for me was while sitting on the floor behind my chair, crying gently yet fully into my arms, so wondrously in Love with Sam; his powerful beauty. That was oh so very beautiful and it felt as such a glorious thing to express it to him after the work.

…and how I’m feeling today is profound as well. Almost like a chiropractic adjustment in a sense… ( a psychic adjustment). The astral place still exists strong all around. I can recall that it took a while for the magic to settle in the first time… or for me to ‘get’ that that’s what it was. This time it’s continuously revelatory as it slowly dissipates into ‘normal’ existence. It’s like having universal memory at my conscious disposal for further wisdom generation… and I’m very busy in process all the while feeling such a blessed and glorious Love coursing through me.

That is part of what makes Daime a valuable addition to existence, and I’m so grateful to have found it on my path.

And to have met you. Your understanding of the Universe is inspiring and I thank you.

Love

MyKL

posts.title: At Home
posts.content:
By this time, laura is more than just curious. She’s interested. When I came back home from the second journey with Daime, she looked into my eyes and said “I gotta get me some of that Love drug!”

I’d been sharing the research I’d been through to that point and expounded excitedly about notions and theories regarding our species’ relationship with the divine. She’d been first hand witness to the ‘enlightenment’ I’d been experiencing over the month-or-so of my experience with Daime.

Her experience with ‘drugs’ is limited, having had one experience with psilocybin mushrooms that left her cautious of the experience. At home, she grew up with a lot of darkness around drugs with a lot of confusion from her parents–and dishonesty. Over the years with she has developed an alliance with Cannabis but is still dealing with feelings of guilt about it.

It seems as though in our society, the only acceptable ‘drugs’ are those sanctioned by the state: alcohol, coffee, tobacco, pharmaceuticals. All are extremely addictive and/or destructive substances.The more ‘connected’ allies like Cannabis and Entheogens such as Ayahuasca, LSD and Psylocibin, are surrounded by an air of uncertainty and fear. In the United States, possession of Cannabis is a considered a crime on par with, if not worse, than murder in terms of the punishment for it.

My impression is that this is clear evidence of the damage of the pharmacratic inquisition… the ‘drug war’. We’re on a pretty destructive path as we continue to spiral through our societal addictions (add sugar, oil and T.V. to the above list of ‘drugs’ sanctioned by the state.) Entheogens and other allies offer us some guidance through this–at least in my experience, and are to be revered as important tools and not suppressed. We as a society are clearly seeing the effects of this prohibition in our world in deep crisis…

So, that laura’s opinion about it has been transforming from fear to acceptance was a really great thing. Especially since it was becoming an important part of my journey. It felt so connecting and warming that I was able to share in this with my Love. Her cautious curiosity was gradually becoming interest… and then excited interest and into desire.

The next time the Daime came, laura agreed that she wanted to participate. It was to be a special work. Mestre Ineru’s birthday celebration: a dance work. A very different ceremony than what I’d already experienced. Of course, I was excited too.

posts.title: Growing
posts.content:
Growing

I can feel it growing
and suppose that you can too:
this web of information
that spreads itself through
precise and clever articulation
through our minds, our beings, going

over and over. It’s folding
over and over and
things are colliding,
becoming, connecting
and always probable.
In motion and
transforming in the whim
of a glance
or some other notice.
Transforming over and over
folding over and over
into what it is telling us
over and over
which
is:

YOU
are essential to all of this while
YOU
are nothing at all…

(…hold me.)

The purpose, I presume,
(though I’ve only got a clue)
is to tell us all a story
of how to break on through

so that
we’re not left
wandering,
wondering what went wrong when we
suddenly

forever lose connection
with this world that we see.

…But only if we feel it,
let this force be free
to manifest
wisdom
over and over and…

(…I’m in it for the Love
So in it for the in Love
Oh so in Love
but wait!…
…What about you?)

posts.title: A Little Bit of Her Story
posts.content:
The Daime, She is a divine being. I’m not certain of where She came or of how She arrived. I’m not even sure of what She wants, though I’ve got some ideas. She’s been communing with indigenous people of ‘South America’ for thousands of years, much like her sisters and cousins in other parts of the world (rue, acacia, datura, psilocybe mushroom, san pedro, salvia divinorum, phalaris…).

She’s been called Daime for less than a century. The Santo Daime church was founded in 1930 by Raimundo Irineu Serra (1892-1971). As a young man he migrated to the rainforest where he spent six years in Acre, working as a rubber tapper.

While learning the trade, he was also learning about spirituality in the jungle. He tried the sacred ayahuasca tea and was shown the proper way to prepare it. He was taught methods for journeying into ecstatic states, and learned how to integrate the visions and knowledge he brought back from those journeys.

Ireneu’s significant vision was of a Divine Lady, sitting in the moon, who told him he must retreat into the forest for eight days with only ayahuasca to drink and only macacheira (boiled manioc) to eat. During this retreat he had visions of the ‘Forest Queen’ who told him that he must start a new faith in which the ayahuasca drink would be central.

He started his new church in Rio Branco, the capital of Acre, in 1930. He was still receiving visions from the Forest Queen and he also chanelled hymns. Collections of these hymns became the church’s guiding principle.

After his passing in 1971, family and disciples continued his work, which constitutes today a true national religious tradition in Brazil. Under the guidance of Padrinho Sebastiao (1920-1990) they founded dozens of churches in Brazil and abroad. Today, there are an estimated 20,000 practitioners of the doctrine in Brazil and many thousands more worldwide.

It is interesting to note that an important psychoactive component of the brew is a chemical called DMT or dymethyltryptamine (daime=dymeth?). DMT was first synthesized in 1931 by British chemist Richard Manske; around the same time that Ireneu was making his discoveries.

posts.title: Third Daime
posts.content:
This was by far the most ‘different’ of all of the ceremonies so far… for several reasons.

For starters, it was a dance work. According to the Daime calendar of works, they happen throughout the year, usually in concert with specific celebrations. This one was celebrating the doctrine’s founder’s birthday.

It was also the first work where Laura would be present and sOMa as well. We were going to church as a family! Something I’d not done in a great many years.

It was strange to also have to be in a caregiver role during something as personal and as intense as a Daime work. We were assured by Horatio that this was common practice in Brazil.

The dance works are different in that the whole ceremony is done while the congregation is standing. Not only that, we’re moving from side to side in a hypnotic and rhythmic yet simple two-step. Side to side. Side to side. Side to side.

After the opening prayers and the first drink of sacrement, it became really clear that sOMa wasn’t too interested in participating. Sam and Tara were not doing the work either as they’d been to one the night before and so felt to hang out in their room for the day while we danced and sang downstairs. Or, I should say They. sOMa and I were not really there either.

So it was wierd. I felt the effects of the first serving kick in while I wasn’t in the ceremony. We hung out upstairs for a little bit and then sOMa and I went home for a snack then a nap.

After he was asleep, I returned to the work in perfect timing for the second serving. I was able to sing and dance for another hour and a half and really connect with the work. The complexity of what is trying to be accomplished in a dance work is really jarring at first. Once one gets familiar with all of the elements involved (singing, dancing, working,…) then I trust it gets a little easier. There were a few times in my dance work where I felt it all click but mostly I felt like I was trying to keep up on some level or another. When I caught the step, it was at the expense of connection with the words of the song, or a particularly rich vision, or vice versa. Quite a skill to cultivate.

When I went to check on sOMa, he was just waking up. Perfect timing all around: We came out from nap just as a break was getting out and returned to the work in time for the third serving.

After that, sOMa expressed that he wanted to be outside. Great! Me too. I’ve felt, through my first two works that outside is the place to be! Inside feels like a foreign element. We put on our boots and sweaters and headed out into the woods. It was a beautiful early winter day. Not too cold. Just a little crisp. The early evening sun was just setting as we got into the forest.

I was struck immediately with how busy it all was. The Magic of the forest was alive with motion that I don’t normally notice. The language that was being spoken in the woods was different than yet included the voice of what I normally hear and can connect with. I was astonished to be able to be connecting with this aspect of things, like I knew that it was normally hidden from me and that I had a new key right now. I wondered to what degree sOMa’s experience in the woods was like this all the time.

We walked into the middle part of the forest between home and the river, where the runoff creek slithered through to the marshy grass. On its way, eventually, to the Ocean. The game we’ve lately been playing, taking our boat over the creek to Owl Island to pick nuts with Nutkin and his cousins (from Beatrix Potter’s- The Tale of Squirrel Nutkin), was fresh still for sOMa and so we played it out and hung about with this scene for a while.

I went through being rapturous to being cold and frumpy, and back again.

It was eerie. I became aware of a sensation of fear inside of me. It felt dark and brooding and lingering in the shadows of my processes. It felt almost like fear had a personna and while I felt that I was safe and that there was no ‘evil’ afoot, there was this issue at hand for me: I was afraid of the effects of the Daime. While I was enjoying playing with sOMa, I was also working very hard to remain conscious. To NOT have happen now what happened the first time I communed with Daime.

Nothing like that happened though.

What did happen is that I got to experience Ayahuasca in a deeply personal setting and on my own, without the guidance of a more experienced traveller. And while I travelled, I noticed, simply by the experience of being in it, how the everything of it all is made out of…. POSSIBILITY. And it’s really fast! About as fast as you can notice.

We made our way out of the woods and back home. While we waited for the work to end, we ate some frozen berries and some dates. When Christian came over from next door, we knew that the work was done and we went over for the post ceremony celebration… for Agape.

It was a celebration. It was this church’s first dance work and the house was jubilant with connection and experience. sOMa was spun around on the floor more than once!! There was much curiosity about my adventures and certainly a wariness from Horatio around the Idea of communing alone. I certainly can understand, with what I noticed out there.

I would say: Or at least, not while in a caregiver role. I’m not so sure I’m not meant to follow in Mestre Ireneu’s footsteps and retreat with Aya as a guide.

And I can certainly understand.

posts.title: The Importance of Family
posts.content:
What a relief! laura’s experience with the Daime was a positive and enriching one. She spent most of the work lying down and ‘traveling the astral’ as she put it. She felt a light, blissful state that was full of process and experience and that she understood the value of experiencing again.

I went into it with some fear. Stories of Horatio and his situation at home. I got the sense that not only was there little support there, but his partner was far removed, by choice, from his experience with Daime. I had hoped that that wouldn’t be my situation.

What a relief to find that it wasn’t. laura understood immediately the magnitude of this for me. While the effects of her first Daime weren’t necessarily AS transformative as mine, they still were profound and transformative. It began a different leg of her path (which includes a LOT of yoga).

It got me to thinking about the importance of community and especially of family in the realm of spirit. We go through life attracting all the pieces of the path that need to be shown to us. I knew about Daime and my journey from the very first communion. The community that exists in Daime is so beautifull and conscious. And now, my family is included. It’s not just me, it’s my family. And it’s further reassurance that I’m on the right path.

My third journey into Daime taught me very clearly the value of community. Being alone while tripping with Daime was not the ideal. Daime needs a community consciously calling her into this realm to affect profound change. I suppose that it can be done solo but the journey was lacking a certain connection.

But now that laura knows what I’m talking about, we can share in this together. Ideas and notions, as they come to me are more readily shared as I can trust that even if she doesn’t quite get it, she’s open to discovering and feels empathic toward any revelation. And the same is true from my end.

A religious experience is an overwhelmingly profound thing. I guess that’s why it’s called religious. Mystical and Otherworldly are not too strong of terms to use either.

When i compare what I’ve experienced with Daime as compared to any other spiritual path I can say that the only place I’ve found a similar connection is in direct communion and experience of nature. And while this Daime experience is comfortably wrapped in a Christian ritual, it’s also steeped heavily in the unknown and in the chaos of the Natural world.

My only real leap of faith in this (as compared to Catholicism which is ALL faith and NO experience), is that i have to trust that this divine being is showing me the truth and light. AND that this truth and light exists inside of me all the time.

This is stuff that we all know intrinsicly, I believe instinctually. And this is what lends to the experience’s potency: it is a real and true connection with the ‘Other’, the ‘Void’, the ‘God’  that exists in all of it.

Now that we’re a family experiencing this together, we’re having a good look at the ‘plan’ and are seeing that it’s going to include communion with nature on a pronounced ritualistic level. We’re going to find this all out for ourselfs…. together. The Love has been pumped up to a whole new level.

And now that she’s here and has seen this with me. It’s a journey that is going to expand and become………….

……….hmmmmm. Now what to do about sOMa. According to Horatio, he’s not allowed to commune until he’s eighteen. It would be different if we were in Brazil, where the church is legal and supported by the state. But here, where it’s illegal, there are still too many issues to permit his communion. And so , his participation in the ceremony is on par with the Catholic ‘religious’ experience until then… and that seems a little lacking and almost fraudulent toward him…
It’s something that we’re working on.

posts.title: A Prayer
posts.content:
A Prayer

In fairness to the birds,
It’s their world we’re tromping through
And birds will be birds… allways… it’s true;
Whatever that may look like
To someone like me or you.

Now in all fairness to we,
It’s our world they’re sharing too
We do belong here, me and you,

In all that this earth brings:
Be it the bounty of bird calls in ecstasy
Or the soaring of wind through wings

If only we could claim what little we really need
Instead of practicing in worship of things.
May we all find the precious balance required
So that ALL may glory in all that’s transpired
And we ALL may fully become one being

On this journey through enlightenment
Whatever exactly that may mean.

posts.title: Fourth Daime
posts.content:
It was a simple hinario work and Horatio prefaced it with assurances that we were all gonna get somewhere because the Daime was strong and we could drink lots of it. Thinking to myself: “Good. I’m looking to get somewhere”.

I ate nothing at all in the morning except for drinking some lemon water. Had four tobacco smokes between waking at eight and the ceremony beginning between 11am and noon. Feeling really good going into it.

After the first drink the effects kicked in suddenly after about 15 minutes and raced intensely to a mild peak over the next hour or so. I say mild but others were experiencing some pretty intense stuff. I was feeling that Horatio was waiting a little too long before the second serving as the peak had clearly passed. I trust though that as an experienced Daimista, he knows.

The second drink was a rapid acceleration back into the ‘peak state’ and then beyond. Feeling like a pillar of energy is connected to a grid of all of us and pulsing (there were 16 this time, 6 women and 10 men). It travelled into some intense swirliness and I took a bit of time on the bed thinking. My mind raced through thoughts about matter and visually I was manifesting flow and convection. Some beautiful knowing serenity

I was reminded by Horatio that being vertical helps me to connect with the light (he came over to check on me… says he does this if he notices we’ve missed three hymns. I like having him around) I think too that maybe he just didn’t want me hogging the mattress. The cozy corners were busy places this work.

When it came time for the third serving Horatio changed the Daime. He brought out some different ayahuasca brew and said that it was made in Hawaii and not in Brazil. I was very intrigued. The difference in flavor was noticeable. Familiar to the other brew but with big sweet overtones, warmth in stead of the cool crispness of the other. Experience-wise, it was different Daime fer sure. This one was a deeper more grounding feeling. I very rapidly became congested in a mucousy way that was a little bit unfamiliar to me.

It didn’t seem like a very long time to the end of the hymns and closing prayers. I was in a daze like state: trying to keep up with the songs and appreciating the boistrous celebratory tone of the group.

When the ceremony it was over I sat still in the daze of it all. We all were jubilant but remained mostly seated and smiling. Offering congratulations and warm Loving energy among us. Beautiful serene collective moment. I connected with Alex, the clarinet player as we felt we needed to acknowledge some of our connectedness throughout the day.

Then when I got out of the room into the foyer of the house, Sam offered for me to join him upstairs to smoke some Santa Maria. It was 6pm when we arrived in his room. We smoked a lot of weed and hung out for about a half hour. Then he gave me some grass to take home.

While he was going down to join the group (who had suddenly burst into full hymns again while we were up there~~really fun to hear!) for some food, I decided I would run back home and put the weed there in a jar instead of having it in my pocket for the evening.

When i stood up, I felt like I could easily jump over the edge, out of my body if i wanted to. I felt woozy in my balance but aware. I saw Jennifer on my way down the stairs and when asked how, I told her that I was feeling very Otherworldly. I slipped on boots and ran outside.

The cold air felt so good. “Yeah outside is where i want to be.” thought I. “I’ll just put on some warm clothes while I’m inside and come out.” I go inside into the dark entranceway of our home and “Whoa swooshyswirly it’s all bumping into me!”….. I am feeling so confused about how to go about this series of sensations. “Do i lay down?…….. How would I go about that? Where?…… I’ve got to put away the grass, that means upstairs. The beanbag chair would be a good place to trip…” My internal dialog is going haywire while I consciously go through the motions of all of the decisions I can possibly make. I manage to stumble up the stairs and then am totally lost for the next hour or so about how to get back outside. I realize the predicament as me being totally trapped in all of the patterned behaviors in my life. It was an amazing exploration. I was conscious of it and frustrated with it and finding it hilarious all at the same time!…

The reason for a lot of the confusion was not being able to use my hands to roll a tobacco smoke. Part of it, I’m attributing this to the Daime not letting me go there. I saw clearly and completely the rationalization that I use inside my mind every day to fool myself into smoking. At the same time, I realized too that it was the same thing for all of it. ALL of it… down to being so programmed to accept the vibration of blue as blue… or the notion of a door and a window and a day.

So after having a lot of fascinating challenges and hilarity…oh, all the while I’m singing a mantra, whispering and making a lot of really cool beat-box type noises that sounded so so so so freaky and psy∞KA∞dellik. the mantra was “just gotta get out side (side side side tchfffa tchffff…..…ae….)“

I finally get outside and the world is still bumping into me. I realize on my way to the back meadow (on the way to the woods…) that the Daime really doesn’t want me to smoke. My body really doesn’t. I’ve only smoked grass and drank Daime and a little water since 11am. I argued with myself for a little bit but finally gave in and smoked.

It really bummed the buzz. The world was still swooshyswirly but there was a depressed tone to the experience. The compounds from tobacco smoke are grounding in a way for me (being a long-time habitual user) but the fact that I let me fool myself was a sad moment of not listening. And I realized that I do it every day. I’ve programmed myself over the last half of my life into a set of behavior that is tremendously self destructive.

But it’s not so self destructive to allow me to pass out outside or to leave my body while I was out there alone on a cold winter night. While the idea of dying doesn’t frighten me anymore, there is a self-preservation thing that is stronger than we realize. I knew too that it would be really bad for the church for me to die. It could hinder its ‘legalization’ as well as its reputation in general, slowing its getting out there…and I believe this needs to get out there, and fast!

I explored noise and vastness while lying in the field and then went back home and lay down on the couch upstairs. I felt that I really wanted to have my own personal stash of Daime to keep me from smoking. I would drink a bit of the brew when the craving hit over the next several days and was convinced that this would cure me of nicotine addiction. I still am convinced that a Daime retreat would be the best thing for me at this point in my life.

I woke up at one in the morning. Everybody was gone from next door. I vaguely remember hearing engines and people leaving. I ate the juice from an orange, smoked some grass and tried to write then went downstairs and slept (laura was at her moms with soma). I woke at 10 and had another orange at around noon. Feeling really good, clean, clear for a fast; but realized that the timing wasn’t… or convinced myself that it wasn’t good. I had to work hard to convince myself to eat eggs.

What a trip! I stayed quite otherworldly and vaguely extra sensory for three or four days. The warmth of the Hawaiian Daime was something and the memory of it as a comfortable presence among the other Daimes I’ve been with stays with me.

posts.title: Fifth Daime
posts.content:
My first communion outside of mine own church. We traveled to Montreal where Padrinho Paulo Roberto was visiting from Brazil. It was also a celebration of the opening of a new church in Montreal: Nova Jerusalem.

This was the busiest of ceremonies yet. There were roughly sixty of us, split pretty evenly men to women. I had been looking forward to a larger congregation after hearing stories of intense current in congregations numbering in the hundreds. I was also excited about being in the presence of such a seasoned daimista as Paulo as well as the regulars from Montreal.

The work was held in a warehouse right downtown. laura and I commuted from the suburbs where we stayed with some good friends who live in a Yoga studio. Padrino opened the work with a talk about spirit and the Daime that was translated into French for those who needed it. Then we prayed and drank the spirit.

The work was a Cura. A healing work. There was a portion of the work also, in which a mediumship work was opened up. Things got pretty crazy with lots of intense dancing and shaking and healing going on. The ‘concentrations’ were deep as there was a wavelike flow hushing over us all and Padrino Paulo would break into hymns seemingly sung in tongue (I don’t know… it’s all portuguese to me.)

It was interesting being in a group where a lot of different egos are melding together for an expereince. A beautifull gathering of explorers working together as well as on their own individual processes. It left me with an excitement to be in Brazil and commune in an even larger congregation.

My relationship with Daime was similar this time as with previous works. She got in and rewired things a little, engaged me in speculative mind’s eye conversation, all the while telling me that sound is a key.

Afterward, it felt strange to have to leave the group. Stranger still to be driving. It’s not that it was challenging to do (though it was in Montreal and we did get lost), it’s that driving seemed such a counterintuitive thing to be doing. I think even that riding in a car would have been an equally strange phenomena for me. But it served its purpose in that it got us back to our son… who was well asleep.

laura’s trip with Daime was very similar to the first. Filled with a lot of light and Love. Lots of moving around on the astral.

The rest of the evening we spent connecting about spirit and Daime and Yoga and…. with the kind friends at the Siva Yoga studio.

posts.title: In-Doctrine-Nation?
posts.content:
By this time, I’m beginning to feel a bit confused about things. Part of this comes, I know, from not rigorously following the Doctrine’s schedule of works. I’ve been playing around with it and not committing to communing every two weeks. The vibe of the Daime in me is potent and ‘omnipresent’–for lack of a better term. At about ten days after a work, I notice a marked drop in my mood. A depression sets in… nothing severe, but noticeable. This is what told me that the two week work schedule made sense. Just as the last one has worn off, it’s time for another communion.

I’m still an outsider in the church. A welcome member to all ceremonies I’ve attended yet not a full fledged fardado. And a good part of my thought process is being spent trying to figure out if being fardado is what I really want.

“What I really want is for what happened to me the first time to happen once again.”

My feelings are that for that to happen again would take a commitment to working at it according to the schedule. Would it? The last two works and the following work have all been according to the Doctrine, but life gets in the way of this being a serious commitment for me. I’m in a place where I’ve found something that I feel passionately to explore but I lack the resources of time and finances to make the leap. Or are those just excuses that I use to hold me back from the possibility of stumbling across something I’m not ready to see? A classic martyrdom playing itself out perhaps.

I’ve come to church five times with the understanding that every time will be different. That certainly is the case on a lot of levels. It feels like a progression of sorts, where I’m learning pieces of a secret code to something. Maybe that space I encountered in my first Daime experience is the ‘reward’ for having completed the work. But… Is the ceremony a necessary part of this process?

Therein lies another question for me. The Santo Daime church has been doing this for about three quarters of a century. Shamans in the jungle have been doing it for millennia. Is what we’re doing in the work really the right way to commune with Ayahuasca? What I’m finding to be work is actually keeping up with what’s going on. That can’t be what everyone is talking about when they say ‘work’, can it?

My feelings are that there are many ‘right’ ways to commune with Aya and that intention is what determines if it is right. So is this right for me? Well, my intent is sacred, and it’s all I’ve got for now. It is what has come and presented itself to me. So, I will continue to explore. I trust that when spirit moves me to try it differently then I will. I have been feeling a lot of inklings about this over the last couple of months.

I’m reminded of Arden. He goes through really heavy emotional purging with every work. He can’t sleep the night before a work and he’s terrified of going into it. But he does it because he has to. It kind of feels like that for me. Now that the gates have been opened I have to find out what it is all about.

I know for certain that entheogens are my spiritual practice. The amount of research I’ve uncovered indicating that plant allies offer THE authentic religious experience, combined with my own personal experience has left no doubt about that with me. What is the word for us? entheogenists?, occultists?, mystics? I like psychonauts a lot, though it doesn’t describe the spiritual aspect fully.

Thus, even if I don’t really understand the purpose of this ceremony, the dance with Daime is worth the work. I’m committed to the work it takes figuring it out, according to how I am able and Daime is currently an apt and most available tool for this…

posts.title: Sixth Daime
posts.content:
I now understand the purpose of the ceremony. I had always known of it but never experienced the connection like in the sixth communion with Daime. Some factors which likely contributed to this connection are as follows:

1. It was a small, intimate gathering of ten people. Six men to four women.

2. I was communing with no-one unfamiliar.

3. For the previous six weeks I had been following the Doctrine’s calendar of communing every two weeks (that part of the Doctrine, I understood already)

5. For the latter part of the work, I was sitting right in the current at the table.

4. It was time for me to understand.

Through this journey with Daime, I have felt a general progression of feelings of ‘enlightenment’. The experience of each work has a certain familiarity to it that is similar to all, but there are always different things presented to me that fit in with my path. Kind of like a series of AHA! moments. It feels as though I’m being transmitted a sacred code of some sort that holds within it the key to something beyond my current ability to fathom. Every communion generates wisdom at an accelerated rate. The profundity of it is especially prevalent in the days following a work, as I integrate the experience with the day-today.

I say this because for most of the work it was “the usual” (i can say that now after this many times) but it got really interesting as the day progressed. When I was welcomed to the table, I experienced a connectedness with the universe that was markedly transcendent.

Everyone sat at the table save for Arden and me. Very quickly after the first serving, the current was established. Arden was sent careening onto the mattress into laborious sobs. “Poor guy”, thought I, and swooned into the flow. He was rescued by Horatio and brought up next to him at the table, leaving me alone on the outskirts of the table… which was fine. I’m a pretty solitary being in a lot of ways, experiencing bliss regardless.

The intensity kept building after the second serving. There were two guitars for the first part but Brice was asked by the Padrhino to refrain from playing for the next hinario so as to concentrate his voice into it. It was at a point when I was feeling especially disconnected and solitary that Horatio brought me to the table. I had stopped singing and was staring, tripping out on the glorious scene in my minds eye. It was effervescent and archaic.

I had been sitting well wrapped in a blanket and feeling cold. The immediate warmth that I felt around the table was inviting and the energy of the space, vibrant and full of light. When we sang, there was a buzz that carried all of our voices into this really strange and mysterious place that was JUST right. I found myself not being conscious of my voice singing the hymns. Like I could pop away from the hymnal psychically for a moment and still remain attached to the flow of the song. I found that unusual Portuguese phrases which I would have expected to trip me up, just rolled out naturally.

At one point when I looked up from the book, during the musical interlude after a hymn, everyone was very clearly in this space with me. Connected right here like a living entity, breathing out the energy of the moment. There was a warm glow over everything which was an orange hue over everyone that was bright and Light and we were all smiling into it and flowing along with it while creating it. Such Magic it was to feel so connected with people.

Then, not long before the third serving there was a sound like someone’s cellphone ringing. It came from high up and sounded like it could have been from the front foyer, just outside the chapel room of the house.

I felt it a little strange at the moment; I thought “Oh well, someone left their phone on.” and then it ‘rang’ again… but it didn’t stop. The ‘ring’ continued for several seconds then the tone changed pitch and a melody was created. It took a little bit for me to realize that it was following along with the tones coming from the guitar. I don’t really know how long I listened to this little song. I don’t recall the moment of it ending. The sound was out of this world. It sounded technological in some way but as though it was made from a technology that wasn’t from here.

I smiled. I smiled such a passionate, fascinated and Lovefull smile.

After the third serving, Brice got back on the guitar and we sped really quickly it seemed, through the rest of it. I found the closing hymns to be especially full of Joy, even though I only sang from memory as I didn’t have a hymnal handy.

After the work we sat for what seemed far longer than usual. Exhausted and elated. I was in awe and still very much tripping. It felt like I could have sat there all evening. It was the first time for me that a work finished and that it was daylight out still. A pretty special treat. Horatio didn’t stay for agape as he’d promised his family he’d be home early.

The after work time is a pretty great time. A bunch of people who’ve just gone through a really intense collective yet personal experience together, come down and commune in a different and less ‘doctrinated’ way. It takes me considerable time after the cereomony to be in a space to connect with people in a coherent way. It seems like every time, my experience would be well served by some quiet meditation after the work, something I’ve yet to completely cultivate. I’m really fascinated though, that people can eat so heartily after a work. I feel called to take it really slow with water and small amounts of fruit and don’t usually eat a meal until the next day.

I found that I couldn’t keep still for the rest of the evening. In the kitchen, after spending some time with Eloise the cat, I found a sunbeam coming through the west-facing window. This discovery moved me to play around with my equilibrium, moving, flowing dancing… and it just didn’t stop. Hours after the ceremony it was still going on. While I enjoyed some thawed blueberries and some slices of grapefruit (which was out of this world!!), I had to keep moving, whether it was tapping my foot, or flexing and releasing my hands, or breaking back into the trance flow equilibrium dance.

It felt so good to be in a space of Love, surrounded by people who felt it all right that my process involved such movement. At one point, I asked the room if anybody had heard the sound that intrigued and pleased me so. Sam and Tara both had. I felt such a tremendous sense of relief. It was real!…in that it was experienced consciously than more than just myself. In effect, the three of us had shared in a sonic hallucination. whoa!(more about this later)

After all had left and Arden had gone up to bed, I was in the kitchen with Sam and Tara. Sam decided he wanted to make waffles… with whipped cream. In a fit of sugar madness, I relieved the whipped cream bowl of a several finger-fulls of the sweet fluffy froth. It was yummy but I felt the mucus build right away in my throat and a moment later the toxic headache began… a lesson in the importance of diet in this for me. Daime is helping to clean out my system. I can feel it on a lot of different levels.

Not long after, I crashed on their couch. I woke at four in the morning with my head pounding so intensely. I went to the bathroom and threw up, then I had a poo. Then I went home and into bed. Well relieved, and transformed yet again through this relationship with a Magical ally called Daime.

posts.title: Morninglory
posts.content:
Morninglory

…fallen leaves spinning
are nothing
if not children running…

**

1.

We would commune here again:

The valley insists upon sharing its secrets
regardless of how deeply one chooses to see,
to hear, to smell, to touch, to be.

We participate each in our own way
in the magic we co-create by being;
simply to allow as it sweeps us away.

The dense multicoloralism of the hillside
is day’s micro-cosmical mirror to the clear-nighted sky:
its starry fractal representation, an expression of a code…

(“There’s mathematics in them thar hills!”)

An oracle:
Love…
that tells us, if nothing else,

that if we can engage more immediately, with more intimacy,
we’ll more clearly remember the language
of its metamorphic symphony.

2.

We would commune here again.

With the popular poplar‘s big frantic and gusty applause
encouraging this comprehension–or the journey at least.
And the ashes, who are already naked, with so much more to say.

Among the welcome and encroaching birches
and the exploding milkweeds
that dance with the breeze, our fresh wishes away.

While the pears are busily shedding their fruit.
And the bur oaks are telling tall tales to the sugar maples.
While the sweetgass is lying down for the day.

3.

We would commune here again.

As community we gather in gratitude, celebration!
and share in connection and growth and play:
the expansion of the Universe unfolding.

As all around us in this beautifull place
we are reminded that it is we who belong to the land
and not the other way around.

**

a child’s laughter is the sunshine;
whispy light at play in the colorful Autumn leaves.

it knows that the stars are magnets
and that a hug is the electric eternity in between.

posts.title: Seventh Daime
posts.content:
Just outside of Grafton, Ontario, there is a place called the Peace Sound Chamber, a place reserved for sacred journeys. It is a structure built according to a vision received by Joseph Rael (Beautiful Painted Arrow) and is one of perhaps sixty in the world. It was in this Magical place that I communed with Daime for the seventh time.

I struggle still, to find words to describe this work. Part of it is expectation (a good lesson), in that it was the seventh communion; somewhat of a benchmark. I had heard that any serious wisdom generating activity needs seven tries to establish itself. I wondered if that elusive and otherwordly space that I craved would be there as a result. The space itself carried with it a certain expectation, being a space designed for sacred activity, not like a room in a house being used for that purpose.

Part of it was being awestruck to distraction with the space upon meeting it. A magnificent piece of work: a dome atop a circular room. Warm fire glowing and walls that are made of crystals. Something to behold…

Whatever it was, it was an extremely different experience. Almost off-putting.

After a preamble by the Padrinho, which described the awesome chamber and its purpose (:to chant for peace, for the dolphins and the whales so that what happened to the buffalo does not happen to them), we communed.

The Daime was different. Very different. Extremely hard to drink, so acrid and crisp. I had to choke it back. So vile was the brew that once in the mouth, swallowing was a battle of the will.

Then as we sang, and the current connected us up and people began to feel their intensities, I sat and felt like I was missing something. There was an inkling of that Daime feeling coming on as we went into the second serving.

Even harder to swallow the potion. And again, negligeable effects. I was feeling some of the physical sensations, but nothing of the mental, or even any sort of connection with the grid. I was really getting into singing though. It felt dutiful to be belting out the hymns, though there was a detachment emotionally from it.

Before the third serving, I stepped out and communed with Santa Maria. Maybe she would set this strangeness straight. It did a little immediately. The third serving was the ‘good-ole-Daime’ again.

This time it came on and set me into that Daime space. Familiar and grounding. But, now we were at the ‘local hymns’ segment of the work. This is where all of the locals who’ve had hymns channeled to them bring them out. On one level it’s really great. It’s witnessing the Doctrine as a living entity, continually in transformation. On the level of my context with the current ceremony however, I would have preferred a full Hinario. The presentation of individual hymns lacked the cohesiveness of a full hymn book as an experience. A series of hymns acts as a tool to affect certain states while communing. I felt through this work like I had missed it until now, and now we were playing something else and I hadn’t got to do my work.

As I said, it was a very strange work. Afterward, at agape, I was particularly disconnected and struggling with trying to understand the strangeness of my journey this time. In speaking wit Horatio just before leaving I was told that I was given a specific job by the Daime and that my adherence to that was my work for the ceremony. I felt I had done a good job despite the confusion. Perhaps the third serving and subsequent lack of focused Hinario was like my little treat for having done so well.

All a lot of unknowns and a disappointing and challenging work after such Magic as the sixth. But all for a purpose, I presume. Definitely a lesson in expectation and attachment to be taken intentfully into every future work. We never know what the Padrinho, and specifically the Daime have in store at a work.

posts.title: Rocks Talk
posts.content:
It was some time later that Sam Tara and I spoke more about the noise we’d heard. We went over it together and described more clearly our memories of the even. I brought up the notion that the sound followed along with the guitar, then began tracing the trajectory of where the sound would go before it hit my ears.

It was a wonderful and sudden realization that one of the first things the sound waves would have hit coming out of the guitar is a huge rose quartz that sat on the table. Could it be that which was making the sound?

The weird technological edge to the sound is not dissimilar in concept to the rigidity of crystallization.

When we consider the theory that psychedelic plant allies or Entheogens act on the body by turning off some of the filters in place allowing us conscious access to some or all of the activities we normally block out in order to survive; things such as the sound of our blood rushing through our veins and other body sounds, the amount of information we can see with our eyes (whether hallucinatory (i.e. the mind’s eye) or real) or what we pick up energetically or pheromonially from other beings around us, we see that the idea of being able to perceive some subtle nuances of sound in this state is not too far fetched.

What it brought up for me was profound appreciation for rocks. The mineral kingdom is filled with many mysterious beings. These formations exist on a time/space level that we probably could never fully grasp and yet they are affecting us in every moment, even to the level of some of their components making up some of what we are… take the calcium out of people and there’s not much to see.

I’m going to be more conscious of the rocks around me. I believe there is much to be learned from these ancient energies.

posts.title: Eighth Daime
posts.content:
Oh what a special Daime!  It was also Good Friday Mass for the dead.  Not normally the type of ceremony held on Good Friday in the Church but a tradition for years in Ceu do Toronto.  It was held at the Peace Sound Chamber.

What made it especially special was the personal circumstance.  Laura’s father had just passed.  She had returned from her ten day journey to seeing him off(he died in her arms) only a few days  prior to the work.

The night before the ceremony I was restless and couldn’t sleep.  Frazzled from a sudden decision to drive Laura and Soma to Grandma’s house which got me back home well after midnight.  I decided to take a shower.  While getting clothes ready for the work, I was called to some of Roy’s clothes that laura had brought back.  He had a white shirt and some really comfortable socks.  I was already wearing his belt and decided to dedicate the work to him.  It was a really emotional shower.

It was an appropriate way to begin the ceremony then.  I wondered if this journey with Daime was to help Roy’s soul get to where it needed to go.  The Mass for the Dead is a beautifull yet sombre ritual. It involves lots of rosaries interspersed with really melancholy, sorrowful hymns.

The Daime was strong.  At one point, well after the mass portion of the work I had to leave the current and huddle next to the fire.  I was feeling a bit cold but also needed to feel myself and breathe.  I still tried to sing but it didn’t work and I closed my eyes.  I felt the shadows of beings swimming around all around me.  It was an interesting feeling and i wondered if laura’s dad was getting there.

Throughout the work, I held a beautifull Brazilian white quartz in my wrist hemp string (that I’ve gotten into the habit of tying with intention before a work) and hand.  Christian brought a bag of them into the Salao so they could share in the energy with him before he tied them to his tipi poles.

I heard briefly, twice near the end of the work, the sound that I can only describe as the rock talk.  I almost thought It wasn’t real then I heard another sound.  Different in its makeup but still resonating with that same ‘technology’.

I felt particularly exhausted after the work.  Slowly I made my way outside and Communed with Cannabis.  It again took me a while to get back to ‘normal enough’ to engage with people.  I chose not to lie down, rather sit cozy by the fire and dream along with Daime, which was still going on strong.

Then, when able it became a wonderful evening of some revelatory connection.  It feels like I’m being shown some methods for using my skills as a creator to affect some positive energy in the world.

Before he left back to Napanee, Christian and I spontaneously exchanged birthday gifts.  Our birthdays are a day and twelve years apart… a fellow Aries Rat. He rooted through his bag of tipi quartz and returned the crystal that I had held through the work.   It felt like home.

posts.title: Tenth Daime
posts.content:
After ten days of no solid food, and a well-cleansed system (so it felt) an opportunity to commune was presented to me.  The work was in the city… my first one there.  It was also the first time I had begun a work after dark.  It was held in a room rented at the back of a little bookstore called Wonderworks on Harbord St. in Toronto.  I’ve shopped there before and it was interesting to be unloading a ceremony into the space and overhear the people working that we were setting up for ‘some Christian thing’… if they only really knew.

The space was not really well suited for what we were doing.  It was too small for the thirty-or-so people in there plus altar, etc.  The single bathroom was mistakenly located on the women’s side of the room and there was not a lot of airflow.  Hot and muggy work.  While preparing for the work and through parts of the ceremony, an intense thunder and lighning storm raged over the city pouring buckets of rain.  I’ll share what happened to this space by relaying what I wrote to a couple of friends afterward.  One of whom is Christian who has been with Daime before:

…Daime last night.  Concentration with Pad. Alex Polari.  Did you go on the weekend? no?  Alex is sweet and gentle but his guitar player um… Christina Maria I think is her name, is completely other-dimentionally OUT of this world.  !!!!  Wholllllly! They’re in Montreal this weekend and I would encourage a trip highly.  The brew is fresh and potent, but Christina Maria on the axe and the presence of her bellowing voice, her beauty, AH!

It was a really difficult work though.  The concentration fell apart after the second serving and the current was huge, yet wobbly.  Folks got bounced around.  People dealing with a lot. Me, I had the incredible challenge of having to hold my anal sphincter the whole time.  I broke once to poo lots and lots of liquid and gunk  and then had to hold it immediately again for the rest of the ceremony.   That’s work!  Afterward, I must have pood a gallon, maybe more until about one this afternoon.  Big buildups and bursts.  Really incredibly cleansing.  It’s not the diahrea, like no pains, just flow.  I farted at one-ish today and that told me things were back to “normal”.  I still haven’t eaten food since the previous daime… 11 or 10 days.  I feel incredible though i’m getting bored with it.  need some variety on the palate.  everybody stinks, their breath.  I find that everyone’s breath is stinky.  Wierd.  I’ve got lots of energy though….

**

Today has been something else. WHOA! last night into today. I found a sitter and went to the Daime work.

The brew was fresh and potent. Because of no digestive obstructions at all, I really felt it moving through my system. Then, the gassy feeling happened (it happened before, one time when I did it, I had the most intense farts ever) but it was more intense than the last time… then I felt a flush, and suddenly my colon was full. Like diahrea. I held it while we sang and went into silent concentration. About halfway through the ceremony I went to the toilet and let it all go, came back and sat down and whoosh! the colon was full again. So in essence, I spent the whole work holding my sphincter.

Then afterward, flush, flush, flush. And today flush flush until about one. I’ve pood about a gallon of liquid I bet. Interesting to note.. it was clear when I went to bed at 3:30, the liquid coming out. And it was gunky this morning. Conclusion: we’re always making shit even if we don’t eat. It wasn’t like diahrea either. There was no pain, just flush. Incredible. ayahuasca has got to be the best laxative going.

**

After all that flushing, i didn’t feel especially weak or dehydrated.  Rather, I was full of energy.  It felt like Daime knew exactly what she was doing and it didn’t feel scary to have such an overwhelming purge.

Such an intense physical experience firmed for me my commitment to Daime, or Ayahuasca as a practice.  I know for sure that there is only good in this and that whatever may feel wierd or uncomfortable or even especially challenging is simply part of the work of being alive.  If the Daime didn’t take care of it in her own special (and usually intense) way then it would have to be done some other way.  In the case of my flush, I suspect that the alternative is festering fecal matter releasing toxins into my system over decades resulting in toxemia induced ‘dis-eases’ of one form or another as my clogged system chugs along.

I keep thinking that if everybody could experience this ally we’d all be so much the better for it.

posts.title: Ninth Daime
posts.content:
For a while, I have known that what I really need to do is to get a fast or cleanse going after a Daime work.  This was the work to kick off my next venture into the “Master Cleanse”; a really great ten-day cleanse.

The work wasn’t going to happen on the date I’d assumed but the Universe tapped into my needs, it seemed and Horatio called it for the day… what a blessing.

There was a tone to the ceremony that was celebratory but the current was strong and almost oppressive.  There were nine of us and we all sat at the table.  It felt like it was a bunch of old friends getting together to play.  An excitement and giddiness.  I felt clear and focused on the task:  preparing myself for the journey of the next couple of weeks.

 While I was looking to the Daime for guidance for my journey, I didn’t receive any concrete messages about it.  The work was kind of like a pep rally for the cleanse in that regard.  A big part of the focus Daime places on me is pattern recognition and so I felt well equipped from the work to jump into a radically different mindset around food and eating.

After the work, I did eat a little bit of blueberries and sucked on some grapefruit… mostly just to replenish electrolites.  The post-work Agape continued the celebratory tone of the ceremony.  It was a gorgeous day at the farm and many fine folks frolicking about among the spring jubilee. 

The Daime space remained for the entirety of the cleanse and it was the most successful Master Cleanse for me yet.  It was so easy to focus on my new diet while keeping up with the day-to-day of keeping a family fed (I’m a homemaker).  The days passed by dreamily and on day ten of the cleanse I was blessed with a sudden opportunity to commune again:

posts.title: Hot Yoga
posts.content:
For four weeks now, on Wednesday evening I’ve taken a yoga class at the studio where laura hangs out. It’s called Moksha yoga and it’s something quite fantastic. The series of postures is accessible enough for yoga beginners (like me) but interesting enough to keep seasoned yogis practicing too. Key to the experience is that it is done in a hot room, like 100oF. What would otherwise be a good series of stretches and breathing becomes an excellent cleansing workout. The heart rate is up and the sweat is pouring down.

This week it was a little different. I’m cleansing. I haven’t eaten solid food in four days (except for about a half-cup of blueberries and a couple of orange slices on Sunday evening after Daime). My ‘food’ has been a lemonade made from water, dark maple syrup, lemon juice and cayenne pepper. That’s it. (see: the master cleanser, by stanley burroughs) Today and into tomorrow is the hump of the cleanse. It’s where I’m going through the most intense adjustment to the new diet, and purging of crap from my system…so i felt pretty crappy for most of the day. I was concerned about doing a class like this, not so much out of feeling too unwell for it, but more that when I have to pee, it comes on quick and mighty.

I was blessed with a rigorous pee before starting. The class was especially hot and humid and no more difficult than other weeks. I actually felt more limber and flexible. I attribute this to a regular practice, even if it is only once a week. I got really hungry about half way through the 90min class though and water wasn’t cutting it.

While yoga is a very spiritual practice, the ‘gym’ yoga that we experience at all the studios popping up (go yoga!) seems not really designed with that in mind, at least not what I’ve noticed… I do know that yoga is an important part of my spiritual journey. The ritual around my wednesday evening is the connection: after i leave the studio, I commune with Cannabis while I wait for the bus. on the bus, I’m transported to such a serene transcendent space. I replenish my stores with some light food (lemonade today: strange, when I ravenously looked at the bottle tonight I was disappointed that it wasn’t something more substantial, like Hemp/nettle pesto and quinoa that I made for laura and Soma the night before. these thoughts vanished as I became nourished and filled by the sweet, tangy, spicy brew) a half-hour later, I walk vigorously for about forty minutes from the bus stop to home. I arrive pooped but feeling incredible!

It’s sacred time for me. The stresses of the day and week evaporate and I can feel the cells in my body clicking into a new paradigm every time. I’ve thought before this that spirituality was the realm of the mind but I’m realizing that the mind is quite dependent upon the body and as the body becomes strong and clean, the ability to connect the mind to spirit grows.

Now, to figure out how to do this every day.

posts.title: Walking Toward You
posts.content:
From this distance… a Knowing.
